description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

First of all, thank you for building Zettlr. It's a really great tool. I have been using LanguageTool (non-premium) with the "picky" setting for my documents, but I've experienced some pretty persistent spurious error messages. For example, LanguageTool complains when I use "non-typographical" quotes or ellipses despite the fact that Pandoc automatically converts these to the appropriate characters. It would be nice to be able to disable either specific errors or categories of errors in the LanguageTool settings.  

### Example Workflow

Zettlr could either automatically disable certain errors that are not relevant (such as those handled automatically by pandoc) or provide a menu option for opting into and out of types/categories of errors. 

### Caveats

I'm not sure there are any caveats. 

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

Yes

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

I haven't looked into this very hard, but I think it should be relatively trivial since LanguageTool's API has options for opting in and out of specific rules and categories of rules. It's just a matter of deciding how, if at all, this feature should be incorporated into Zettlr.

summary: Rename and Refactor option is missing from context menu

description.text:
### Description

Rename and Refactor option is missing from context menu on Windows 10 (v3.6.0)

When I right-click on a file name in the left-hand panel, the context menu does not show a 'Refactor' option

Troubleshooting Steps I've Already Taken:
- Reinstalling Zettlr.
- Clearing the FSAL cache via Help menu.
- Manually deleting the %appdata%\Roaming\Zettlr folder.
- Verifying that the filename is correctly formatted with both an ID and a title separated by ' - '.
- Confirming that my Zettelkasten ID regex is the default (\d{14}).

### Reproducing

- I create a new note with a timestamp-only filename (e.g., 20230501103025.md)."
- I manually rename the file to include a title (20230501103025 - Test Note.md).
- I restart Zettlr.
- I right-click on the renamed file in the left-hand sidebar.

Expected Behavior: The context menu should show 'Rename and Refactor'.

Actual Behavior: The context menu only shows basic options like 'Rename file' and 'Delete file'. The 'Rename and Refactor' option is not present.


### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description Check for updates using "Help"-> "check for updates" and I get the following screen: The "last checked: never" makes me a bit suspicious as in previous versions there would be a date. So I click on "check for updates" and get this: closing the window gives an additional pop-up message: I don't know whether the check for updates actually fails or whether this is just the case cause there is no update. ### Reproducing Go to "Help"-> "check for updates". Alternatively, start the app. I noticed now that the same black pop-up "cannot check for updates" pops up when starting the application. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0 ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version ubuntu 24.04 LTS ### Additional Information _No response_

summary: Cannot close unsaved file with mouse

description.text:
### Description

I made changes to a file but did not yet save these changes. I attempt to close the tab containing the file using my mouse and the "x" symbol next to the filename. The shown dialogue does not accept any mouse input. I can choose an option with the arrow keys and enter. I can exit the dialogue window by using the "esc" key.
This does not happen when attempting to close the file with the "ctrl+w" combination. The dialogue that appears then accepts mouse input as well as keyboard input. Visually, the window is a bit different as the latter version has an option highlighted. When closing a tab with the mouse, the dialogue does not have any  option highlighted.

### Reproducing

1. open a file (can be empty file)
2. make any changes
3. do not save the changes
4. try to close the file using the "x" icon next to file name in the tab
5. cannot click any of the options, can only choose with keyboard

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

ubuntu 24.04 LTS

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description

Currently, the context menus in the editor are a bit amiss. There are various context menus that are controlled decentrally by several parts of the application. For example, there is one context menu entirely dedicated to links that allow you to open or copy the link target; there is one for citations allowing you to open any Zotero attachments, and so on.

If there is no special widget under the cursor when the user summons the context menu, the editor will show a default context menu with options to format and mark up the text under the cursor.

This is a bit stupid, because this leads to some context menus being very small, while the default context menu is large. In addition, sometimes one may want to format some text in citations or links, or view a Zotero attachment if the citation renderer is off.

### Proposed Changes

I propose to unify the editor context menu. This involves pulling together in a central place all the various context menus and create a single, larger context menu from this. To do so, I propose creating a new function/command, which receives the exact position of the right-click and the editor view instance.

It then continues to use this information to actually retrieve meaningful context from the editor view based on where the user clicked. This includes, e.g., information on the parsing node under the cursor, any selections, and then determine, based on this, what information to show in the context menu.

This is particularly useful in the new table editor. Right now, we have a very weird situation, in which a user who has activated the TableEditor, will be presented with all the new goodies that we've implemented for managing tables, but they have no chance of actually changing any text mark up in this instance, because while the table editor context menu includes numerous table commands, it completely lacks all of the text formatting commands.

Likewise, a user who has the TableEditor disabled will have all the text formatting commands, but none of the table commands.

### Caveats

The only caveat I can think of is where some contexts may have countervailing requirements. I would propose to just get started and see where it leads us.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

Yes

### Zettlr Version

Right off the develop branch

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

any

### Additional Information

Already have some suggestions? Drop them below.

### Description

The LanguageTool integration in Zettlr is incredibly useful. It would be great if the error popover could be invoked using a keyboard shortcut. That would eliminate the need for switching to mouse to see the tooltip for every error highlight.

### Proposed Changes

Something like `Ctrl+.` which opens the LanguageTool popover would be ideal.

### Caveats

summary: [develop] Cursor position reset to beginning of file when switching tabs

description.text:
### Description

I have tried out the recent [refactor: Only use one MainEditor component](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/commit/7c7a7c4adc79698c6c7b5d43d305d21577bf5bf6) commit, and, first of all, it really improves performance a lot! On my work machine ([HP EliteBook 850 G8](https://support.hp.com/ch-de/document/c06982546), Windows 11), I would always get severe lags when saving a file (up two 2 sec). With this new patch, the lags are pretty much gone. Great work, @nathanlesage!

There is one minor issue. When switching tabs, the cursor is always reset to the first character of the file being switched to.

### Reproducing

1. Open two tabs (tab A and tab B)
2. Place the cursor somewhere (but not the first character of the file) in tab A
3. Switch to tab B
4. Switch back to tab A -> Cursor will be at first character

### Zettlr version

develop

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

## Description This PR restyles some of the popovers in the toolbar to increase element spacing. ## Changes The `tags`, `export`, and `pomodoro` popovers were restyled to increase spacing between elements. The export popover had its elements wrapped in a `div` so that the styling was applied only to the popover. The pomodoro start and stop buttons were wrapped in a `div` so they could be centered and widened. ## Additional information # Current # PR --- Tested on: macOS 15.6 and Fedora 42
